trials.reason: B orders frontend components so definitions precede their uses (history-service before ActivityFilter, ActivityItemChangePreview before ActivityItem, ActivityItem before HistoryView and ActivityView) and culminates in ActivityView, the main feature, whereas A buries HistoryView after ActivityView despite ActivityItem already depending on it.

Book B groups the data layer (utils.ts, history-service.ts) before the components that consume it, keeps the nested ActivityItemChangePreview/ActivityItem pair adjacent, and ends with ActivityView.svelte as the culminating integration point, whereas Book A splits history-service.ts away from ActivityFilter/ActivityItem with an unrelated component in between and buries the top-level view before HistoryView.

Book A keeps the tightly-coupled Activity components (Filter, Item, View) contiguous and defers the more peripheral HistoryView tweak to the end, whereas Book B interrupts that same cluster by wedging HistoryView between ActivityFilter and ActivityView, breaking concern locality.

metadata.note: single-family judge (Claude): self-preference caveat applies; generator/judge ids must differ where possible
